prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "单腿硬拉抬膝" (single-leg deadlift with knee drive) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the top of a single-leg deadlift to knee drive: right leg standing with a slight bend at the knee; left knee driven up high in front of the body to hip height with thigh parallel to the floor and shin perpendicular (90 degree knee bend); torso upright with neutral spine; arms swinging in opposition (right arm forward, left arm back) like a running stride; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: gluteus maximus and hamstring of the supporting (right) leg, hip flexors (psoas and rectus femoris) of the lifted (left) leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: core muscles (transverse abdominis) and erector spinae. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "臀大肌 / 腘绳肌" pointing to the glute and hamstring of the supporting leg; "髂腰肌" pointing to the hip flexors of the lifted leg; "核心 / 竖脊肌" pointing to the core and erector spinae area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
